concepts.name: Cloud ML
concepts.description: A deployment paradigm that aggregates massive computational resources in datacenters, constrained primarily by network latency.

concepts.name: Edge ML
concepts.description: A deployment paradigm that moves computation to local networks to achieve sub-100 ms latency and preserve data privacy.

concepts.name: Mobile ML
concepts.description: A deployment paradigm integrating machine learning directly into portable devices, constrained by battery life and thermal limits.

concepts.name: TinyML
concepts.description: A deployment paradigm for microcontrollers operating under extreme milliwatt power budgets and kilobyte memory limits.

concepts.name: Single-Node Stack
concepts.description: A four-layer architectural model consisting of application, framework, OS, and hardware that defines single-machine ML execution.

concepts.name: Light Barrier
concepts.description: The absolute minimum latency floor dictated by the speed of light over network distances.

concepts.name: Power Wall
concepts.description: The thermodynamic limit on how much computation can occur in a given volume before heat causes hardware throttling.

concepts.name: Dennard Scaling
concepts.description: A classical physical law where the relationship between power and clock frequency was cubic, which forced the industry toward multi-core parallelism.

concepts.name: Memory Wall
concepts.description: The widening performance gap between processor compute capacity and memory bandwidth, making data movement the dominant energy cost.

concepts.name: Iron Law of ML Systems
concepts.description: An equation decomposing total ML execution latency into data movement, computation, and fixed overhead costs.

concepts.name: Bottleneck Principle
concepts.description: The principle that in pipelined execution, system throughput is determined strictly by the single slowest operation.

concepts.name: Pipelined Execution
concepts.description: A hardware technique that overlaps data movement with computation to maximize throughput.

concepts.name: Memory-Bound Workload
concepts.description: A workload where execution speed is strictly limited by memory bandwidth rather than processor calculation speed.

concepts.name: Compute-Bound Workload
concepts.description: A workload where execution speed is strictly limited by the processor's calculation throughput.

concepts.name: Arithmetic Intensity
concepts.description: The ratio of mathematical operations performed per byte of data loaded from memory.

concepts.name: Compute Beast
concepts.description: A workload archetype characterized by high arithmetic intensity, typically bound by raw computational throughput.

concepts.name: Bandwidth Hog
concepts.description: A workload archetype that spends more time loading data than computing, constrained heavily by memory bandwidth.

concepts.name: Sparse Scatter
concepts.description: A workload archetype with irregular memory access patterns and poor cache locality, constrained by memory capacity and access latency.

concepts.name: Tiny Constraint
concepts.description: A workload archetype operating under extreme power envelopes and kilobyte memory limits, constrained by device physical limits.

concepts.name: Data Gravity Invariant
concepts.description: The principle that as data scales, the cost of moving it to compute eventually dominates all other costs.

concepts.name: Data Locality Invariant
concepts.description: The rule stating data must remain local when transmission time exceeds total remote processing and network latency.

concepts.name: Depthwise Separable Convolutions
concepts.description: An algorithmic optimization technique used to drastically reduce the mathematical operations required for mobile vision models.

concepts.name: Neural Processing Unit
concepts.description: A specialized hardware accelerator integrated into mobile and edge devices to execute ML tasks with high energy efficiency.

concepts.name: Train-Serve Split
concepts.description: A hybrid architecture pattern where computationally intensive training occurs in the cloud while inference runs locally.

concepts.name: Hierarchical Processing
concepts.description: A hybrid architecture pattern where data flows upward and is progressively processed across tiny, edge, and cloud tiers.

concepts.name: Progressive Deployment
concepts.description: A hybrid architecture pattern where a massive cloud model is systematically compressed into smaller versions for edge and mobile tiers.

concepts.name: System Entropy
concepts.description: The continuous statistical degradation of deployed model accuracy as real-world data drifts away from the training distribution.

concepts.name: Degradation Equation
concepts.description: A formal model quantifying how fast a system's quality decays relative to the magnitude of distributional data shift.

concepts.name: Amdahl's Law
concepts.description: A formula establishing the hard mathematical limit on system speedup based on the fraction of the workload that can actually be optimized.

concepts.name: Model Quantization
concepts.description: The process of reducing the numerical precision of model weights to decrease memory usage and improve inference speed.

concepts.name: Operator Fusion
concepts.description: A memory optimization technique that combines multiple operations to avoid intermediate memory reads and writes.

concepts.name: Activation Checkpointing
concepts.description: A memory optimization technique that trades compute for memory by recomputing intermediate states instead of storing them.
